category_title: Students with persistent wariness and environmental sensory preoccupations
student_description: Student C is highly anxious and easily distracted by environmental sensory preoccupations (touching surfaces, following floor markings) and wariness of a therapy dog. Their capacity to engage is severely limited by these ritualistic behaviors and fears. They uniquely require physical reassurance (proximity, holding hands) and a highly non-directive, child-led approach to feel safe enough to participate.

criteria.rationale: Allowing the student to control the session content maximizes their motivation to take part and reduces anxiety.
criteria.check: The long-term plan must explicitly allow the student to choose and direct the activities, avoiding any imposed or prescheduled framework.

criteria.rationale: A gentle, low-pressure approach is key to long-term engagement for this highly anxious student.
criteria.check: The long-term plan must establish a consistent communication style focused on commenting and suggesting rather than questioning and instructing.
